name: ZincSearch Full-Text Search API
what_it_does: Lightweight open-source full-text search engine with REST API compatible with Elasticsearch APIs — written in Go with minimal resource requirements, making it suitable for self-hosted deployments where Elasticsearch is too heavy.
use_cases: Building search functionality into applications without Elasticsearch overhead
Indexing and querying documents programmatically via Elasticsearch-compatible API
Self-hosted log search for small to medium deployments without Elastic Stack costs
Prototyping search features before committing to full Elasticsearch deployment
Building lightweight search backends for development and staging environments
not_for: Large-scale production search at Elasticsearch or Solr scale (limited clustering)
Complex analytics queries requiring Kibana-level aggregation features
Production enterprise search requiring commercial support
best_when: An agent needs self-hosted full-text search with Elasticsearch-compatible API but much lower resource requirements for small to medium scale.
avoid_when: You need Elasticsearch's full aggregation power, clustering, or commercial support at scale.

auth.notes: HTTP Basic auth with admin credentials. No token-based auth. User management available for multi-user setups. Self-hosted — credentials configured in install.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Apache 2.0 open source — completely free; no limits
pricing.paid_tiers: No paid tier — open source only
pricing.notes: Apache 2.0 license — fully free. Only cost is infrastructure. Single binary deployment — extremely low resource usage. 100MB RAM can serve millions of documents.

performance.rate_limits: No rate limits — self-hosted; limited by server hardware

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API documented. Elasticsearch-compatible API means existing Elasticsearch clients work. Error responses follow Elasticsearch format. No aggregation complexity — simpler query model.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Document indexing idempotent by document ID. Search queries are idempotent reads. Index creation idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Not all Elasticsearch APIs are supported — check compatibility for advanced queries
Limited clustering support — single-node for most use cases
Basic auth only — credentials in request headers on every call
Community project — maintenance and security patches depend on contributors

security_score_components.security_notes: Apache 2.0 open source. Basic auth only. TLS depends on reverse proxy configuration. Self-hosted means full data control. No formal security certifications. Community-maintained — audit code before production use.
